Course Title: AI in Research and Scientific Integrity
Category: Technology & Telecommunications: Information Technology, Science & Technology, Software, Hardware, Telecom
Who is this for: Researchers, Data Analysts, Research Assistants, Graduate Students, Academic Staff, Scientists, R&D Engineers, Market Research Analysts, Clinical Research Coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Research and Development, IT, Academic Affairs, Quality Assurance, Compliance, Data Science, Product Development
Description: Risks of incorrect AI-assisted analysis or fabricated outputs.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with research methods and a general interest in how artificial intelligence tools are being used in academic or scientific settings is all that is required to begin this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with AI-assisted research analysis and explain how fabricated or incorrect AI outputs can compromise scientific integrity. You will also apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI-generated content and recognise the ethical responsibilities researchers hold when incorporating AI tools into their work.
